truthSpec.title: Notification Router
truthSpec.overview: Build a notification routing system that sends notifications through appropriate channels based on priority level, with user opt-out support.

requirements.text: Accept a notification with: userId, priority (critical/high/medium/low), message
Route critical → SMS + email + push, high → SMS + email, medium → email + push, low → push only
Accept user preferences with opted-out channels
Users can opt out of channels but cannot add channels beyond their priority level
Return the list of channels the notification was actually sent through
If all channels are opted out, return empty array (notification is silently dropped)

edgeCases: User opts out of all channels → empty send list
User opt-out for a channel not in their priority level has no effect
Unknown priority level should throw an error
Missing user preferences → use defaults (no opt-outs)
truthSpec.testSummary: 6 tests covering: critical routing, high routing, medium/low routing, opt-out behavior, cannot-add-channels rule, and all-opted-out scenario.

aiBrief.title: Notification Router
aiBrief.overview: Build a notification routing system that sends notifications through appropriate channels based on priority.
requirements: Accept a notification with: userId, priority (critical/high/medium/low), message
Route critical → SMS + email, high → SMS + email, medium → email + push, low → push only
Accept user preferences for notification channels
Return the list of channels the notification was sent through

flaws.description: Brief says critical → SMS + email (missing push); truth says critical → SMS + email + push
The critical channel list in the brief is an obvious typo that matches high — may lure AI into treating critical same as high
Brief mentions 'user preferences' but doesn't specify opt-out semantics or the cannot-add-channels rule
